ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general congee/rice porridge class. No specific Filipino arroz caldo with sausage and egg entry exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers rice porridge generically. No specific Filipino arroz caldo code exists; closest_parent applied.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of Filipino rice porridge with sausage and egg, approximately 350 g, as typically served at a café or casual dining establishment.

nutrient_source.title: Arroz Caldo (Rice Porridge with Chicken) — adapted to sausage-and-egg variant using USDA FoodData Central component data for pork sausage, egg, and rice porridge
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods) with FAO/INFOODS SEA reference for rice porridge base
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for Filipino arroz caldo with pork sausage and egg. Values estimated by weighted component analysis: white rice porridge base (~65% by weight, USDA FDC #168878 cooked white rice adapted to porridge dilution), pork breakfast sausage (~20% by weight, USDA FDC #167904), hard-boiled egg (~10% by weight, USDA FDC #173424), aromatics and fish sauce (~5% by weight). Per-100g values derived from weighted blend. Confidence: medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white rice porridge / congee analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Filipino arroz caldo with sausage and egg. GI of 72 is based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white rice porridge / congee (GI ~72, high category), which is the dominant carbohydrate component. The addition of protein (egg, sausage) and fat may modestly lower the effective GI in practice, but the base GI of 72 is used conservatively. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (72 × 40.25) / 100 ≈ 29.0 for a 350 g serving (11.5 g carb per 100 g × 3.5 = 40.25 g carb per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~320 mg per 100 g; ~1120 mg per 350 g serving) from fish sauce, sausage, and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size or request reduced fish sauce and sausage, as a standard 350 g serving may contribute approximately 1120 mg sodium — nearly half the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 72) from white rice porridge base, with a high glycemic load (~29) per standard serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this dish in small portions and pair it with additional non-starchy vegetables or protein to blunt the postprandial glucose response, given the high GI of the white rice porridge base and a glycemic load of approximately 29 per standard bowl.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~1.3 g per 100 g) and elevated sodium from processed sausage and fish sauce
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should be mindful of the saturated fat contribution from pork sausage and the high sodium load from fish sauce and seasoning, and consider requesting leaner protein substitutes or reduced seasoning.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Filipino Rice Porridge with Sausage and Egg (also known as arroz caldo or lugaw with longganisa and itlog) is a hearty Filipino comfort dish consisting of white rice slow-cooked in a ginger- and garlic-infused broth until it reaches a thick, creamy congee-like consistency. It is topped with sliced pork sausage (longganisa), a boiled or poached egg, crispy fried garlic, sliced spring onions, and a drizzle of fish sauce or calamansi juice. The dish has a savoury, mildly gingery flavour with a soft, smooth texture from the broken-down rice grains. In the Philippines, arroz caldo is traditionally eaten as a breakfast or merienda (snack) dish and is considered a restorative food, often served to the sick or elderly. In Singapore, it is available at Filipino cafés and casual dining spots. Nutritionally, the dish is moderate in energy (~98 kcal per 100 g), provides meaningful protein from the egg and sausage (~5.2 g per 100 g), and is relatively high in sodium (~320 mg per 100 g) due to fish sauce and processed sausage. The white rice base gives it a high glycemic index (~72), making portion control important for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans due to the pork sausage, egg, and fish sauce.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Filipino arroz caldo with pork sausage and egg was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values were estimated by weighted component analysis using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked white rice, pork sausage, and hard-boiled egg. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for white rice porridge/congee. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data (SR Legacy): cooked white rice (FDC #168878), pork breakfast sausage (FDC #167904), hard-boiled egg (FDC #173424), weighted at approximately 65/20/10/5 percent respectively. No exact match found in HPB Singapore Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA for this specific dish. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component sources used for this estimated blend. 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component sources used for this estimated blend. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component sources used for this estimated blend. 5. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0; this is a confirmed near-zero value for the component foods per USDA FDC data. 6. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on white rice porridge/congee from the University of Sydney GI Database; the protein and fat from sausage and egg may modestly reduce the effective postprandial GI in practice. 7. Halal status is marked false as longganisa (Filipino pork sausage) is typically pork-based and not halal-certified unless specified. 8. The dish is sold as 'Special Lucky Porridge' at Lucky Cafe - North Canal Road, Singapore (field survey listing), priced at SGD 11.00. 9. Serving size of 350 g is an estimate for a standard café bowl; actual restaurant portion may vary.
